types.name: ActionArgumentInput
types.description: A JSON-serializable argument entry for a multi-agent action trigger.

inputFields.name: agentCall
inputFields.description: Defines a nested agent call if this argument should trigger an agent interaction.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The argument property name.

inputFields.name: utilCall
inputFields.description: Defines a nested utility call if this argument should trigger a system utility interaction.

inputFields.name: valueDict
inputFields.description: Defines a list of key-value pairs if this argument should be a dictionary.

inputFields.name: valueList
inputFields.description: Defines a list of values if this argument should be an array.

inputFields.name: valueLiteral
inputFields.description: Static literal value if not dynamically bound.

inputFields.name: valuePath
inputFields.description: JSON Pointer referencing the shared Blok state to inject into this argument slot dynamically.

types.name: ActionDependencyInput
types.description:
A dependency for a implementation. By defining dependencies, you can
    create a dependency graph for your implementations and actions

inputFields.name: actionKey
inputFields.description: The key of the state this dependency corresponds to. (i.e frame:acquireimage)

inputFields.name: allowInactive
inputFields.description: Allow inactive nodes, defaults to true

inputFields.name: app
inputFields.description: Which app this dependency corresponds to (i.e. do you want to use a stardist agent for that or imagej agents needs to be a world unique classsifier (reverse domain notation) that identifies the type of agent you want to use, and then we can have multiple agents of the same type running in the system, e.g. startdist could be the app for all agents that correpsond to a startdist instance)

inputFields.name: argMatches
inputFields.description: The demands for the action args, this can be additionaly specified so that when we loosen the matching criteria for an action in a resolver, we can still make sure to match the right action based on the demands for the args. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the action. This can describe the action and its purpose.

inputFields.name: forceArgLength
inputFields.description: Require that the action has a specific number of args. When loosing the matching criteria for an action in a resolver, we can still make sure to match the right action based on the demands for the args. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: forceReturnLength
inputFields.description: Require that the action has a specific number of returns. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key of the action. This is used to identify the dependency in the system.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the action. This is used to identify the action in the system.

inputFields.name: optional
inputFields.description: Whether the dependency is optional or not. If the dependency is optional, the agent doesn't have to provide it to be potentially callable

inputFields.name: protocols
inputFields.description: The protocols that the action is implementing or relying on. This is used to identify the demand in the system, and can be used to match actions that are implementing the same protocol together.

inputFields.name: returnMatches
inputFields.description: The demands for the action returns, this can be additionaly specified so that when we loosen the matching criteria for an action in a resolver, we can still make sure to match the right action based on the demands for the returns. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: The version of the action this dependency corresponds to.

types.name: ActionHash
types.description: The `ArrayLike` scalar type represents a reference to a store previously created by the user n a datalayer

types.name: ActionKind
types.description: The kind of action.

types.name: ActionScope
types.description: The kind of action scope.

types.name: AgentCallInput
types.description: Defines a callback that routes user interactions directly to an Arkitekt Agent via Rekuest.

inputFields.name: arguments
inputFields.description: Key-value arguments map compiled for the target agent call.

inputFields.name: dependency
inputFields.description: The abstract agent dependency key declared in the Blok manifest (e.g., 'stage_dep').

inputFields.name: operation
inputFields.description: The target function name registered on that specific agent's worker thread loop.

types.name: AgentDependencyInput
types.description:
A dependency for a implementation. By defining dependencies, you can
    create a dependency graph for your implementations and actions

inputFields.name: actionDemands
inputFields.description: The action demands of the agent. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: app
inputFields.description: Which app this dependency corresponds to (i.e. do you want to use a stardist agent for that or imagej agents needs to be a world unique classsifier (reverse domain notation) that identifies the type of agent you want to use, and then we can have multiple agents of the same type running in the system, e.g. startdist could be the app for all agents that correpsond to a startdist instance)

inputFields.name: assignPolicy
inputFields.description: The policy used to pick which instance of the agent to assign to.

inputFields.name: autoResolvable
inputFields.description: Whether this dependency is auto resolvable or not. If so we will try to automatically resolve it based on the demands specified in the dependency and the capabilities of the available agents in the system. This is used to identify the demand in the system. Attention if any of the dependencies of this agent dependency is not auto resolvable, this dependency will also not be auto resolvable

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: A description of the dependency, why it is needed and what it is used for. This can be used to provide more context to users when assigning dependencies.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key of this dependency, when assigning you can reference this key to specify which agent_dependency you are assigning to.

inputFields.name: maxViableInstances
inputFields.description: The maximum amount of viable instances for the agent. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: minViableInstances
inputFields.description: The minimum amount of viable instances for the agent. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: mutuallyExclusiveKeys
inputFields.description: A list of keys of other agent dependencies that are mutually exclusive with this one. This means two agent dependencies with mutually exclusive keys cannot be assigned to the same implementing agent. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the agent. This is used to identify the agent in the system.

inputFields.name: optional
inputFields.description: Whether the dependency is optional or not. If the dependency is optional, users can choose to not provide it

inputFields.name: preferedInstances
inputFields.description: The prefered amount of instances for the agent. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: stateDemands
inputFields.description: The state demands of the agent. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: The version of the app this dependency corresponds to.

types.name: AnyDefault
types.description: The `ArrayLike` scalar type represents a reference to a store previously created by the user n a datalayer

types.name: App
types.description: An application, identified by a globally unique, reverse-domain identifier (e.g. live.arkitekt.app).

fields.name: identifier
fields.description: The globally unique, reverse-domain identifier of the app.

types.name: AppImageInput
types.description: Input describing a built app image to register (its manifest, image, selectors and inspection).

types.name: Arg
types.description: The `Arg` scalar type represents a an Argument in a Action assignment

types.name: ArgPortInput
types.description:
Port

    A Port is a single input or output of a action. It is composed of a key and a kind
    which are used to uniquely identify the port.

    If the Port is a structure, we need to define a identifier and scope,
    Identifiers uniquely identify a specific type of model for the scopes (e.g
    all the ports that have the identifier "@mikro/image" are of the same type, and
    are hence compatible with each other). Scopes are used to define in which context
    the identifier is valid (e.g. a port with the identifier "@mikro/image" and the
    scope "local", can only be wired to other ports that have the same identifier and
    are running in the same app). Global ports are ports that have the scope "global",
    and can be wired to any other port that has the same identifier, as there exists a
    mechanism to resolve and retrieve the object for each app. Please check the rekuest
    documentation for more information on how this works.

inputFields.name: children
inputFields.description: The child ports (used for list, dict, union and model ports).

inputFields.name: choices
inputFields.description: The options for the port. This is used for dropdowns and text inputs

inputFields.name: default
inputFields.description: The default value for the port.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the port. This is the text that is displayed in the UI when the user hovers over the port

inputFields.name: effects
inputFields.description: The effects of the port

inputFields.name: identifier
inputFields.description: The identifier of a structure port. This is used to uniquely identify a specific type of structure.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key of the port

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of the port. This is the type of the port. Can be either int, string, structure, list, bool, dict, float, date, union or model

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: The label of the port. This is the text that is displayed in the UI

inputFields.name: nullable
inputFields.description: Whether the port is nullable or not. If the port is nullable, it can be set to null. If the port is not nullable, it cannot be set to null

inputFields.name: requires
inputFields.description: The descriptors for the port. Descriptors are key-value pairs that can be used to add additional metadata to a port. When using rekuest's action search, you can filter actions based on their port descriptors

inputFields.name: validators
inputFields.description: The validators for the port

inputFields.name: widget
inputFields.description: The assign widget to use for this port.

inputFields.name: asParagraph
inputFields.description: Whether to display the input as a paragraph or not. This is used for text inputs and dropdowns

inputFields.name: choices
inputFields.description: The choices to display in the dropdown. This is used for dropdowns and text inputs

inputFields.name: dependencies
inputFields.description: The dependencies of the assign widget, which will be passed to the search or the hook widget. Use the .. syntax to traverse the tree of ports. For example, if you have a port with the key 'foo' and you want to reference a port with the key 'bar' that is a child of 'foo', you would use 'foo..bar'

inputFields.name: dependency
inputFields.description: The dependency that we are going to use to fullfill the state choices. If none is provided its the own state that will be queried

inputFields.name: fallback
inputFields.description: The fallback assign widget to use if the current one fails. This is used for custom assign widgets

inputFields.name: filters
inputFields.description: The filters to apply to a search widget. This is used for custom assign widgets

inputFields.name: followValue
inputFields.description: The key of another port whose value this widget should follow and mirror.

inputFields.name: hook
inputFields.description: The hook to run when the input is changed. This is used for custom assign widgets

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of the assign widget. Can be either dropdown, text, slider, checkbox, radio or custom

inputFields.name: max
inputFields.description: The maximum value of the slider (if a slider). This is used for sliders and text inputs

inputFields.name: min
inputFields.description: The minimum value of the slider (if a slider). This is used for sliders and text inputs

inputFields.name: placeholder
inputFields.description: The placeholder of the input. This is used for text inputs and dropdowns

inputFields.name: query
inputFields.description: The query to run when searching for choices. This is used for dropdowns and text inputs

inputFields.name: stateAccessors
inputFields.description: State accessors are used to specify how to access the state values that we are going to use to fullfill the state choices. This is used when the state value that we want to use is not the same as the one of the port, e.g. when we want to use a specific key of a state object, or when we want to use a dynamic key based on the other arguments. The option_key field is used to specify which part of the state accessor we want to use as the value for the assign widget (e.g. the key, the description, the logo, etc.)

inputFields.name: stateChoices
inputFields.description: The key of a state whose value provides the choices for this widget (state-driven choices).

inputFields.name: statePath
inputFields.description: The path to the state value that we are going to use to fullfill the state choices. Always traverse from top to bottom level. i.e state.x for state.x and state.x.y for state.x.y. You can also use an arrow function to specify a dynamic path based on the other arguments, e.g. (args) => state[args.foo]

inputFields.name: step
inputFields.description: The step value of the slider (if a slider). This is used for sliders and text inputs

inputFields.name: targetAction
inputFields.description: The action that we are going to target with a proxy widget. This is used for proxy widgets

inputFields.name: targetDependency
inputFields.description: The dependency that we are going to target with a proxy widget. This is used for proxy widgets

inputFields.name: targetPort
inputFields.description: The port that we are going to target with a proxy widget. This is used for proxy widgets

inputFields.name: ward
inputFields.description: The ward that is responsible for handling querying the choices

types.name: AssignWidgetKind
types.description: The kind of assign widget.

types.name: Backend

fields.name: name

fields.name: organization

fields.name: pods
fields.description: The pods currently running on this backend.

fields.name: resources
fields.description: The resources declared on this backend.

fields.name: user
fields.description: The user that registered this backend.

types.description: Filter for backends.

inputFields.name: ids
inputFields.description: Keep only backends whose ID is in this list.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Case-insensitive search on the backend name.

types.name: BlokImplementationInput
types.description: Which locks does the agent provide in general

inputFields.name: catalog
inputFields.description: The optional catalog name if this Blok should be registered inside a specific namespace in your Electron app's UI component registry.

inputFields.name: components
inputFields.description: The UI component tree blueprint for this Blok.

inputFields.name: demoState
inputFields.description: An optional JSON-serializable object providing demo state values for this Blok's internal reactive data model, useful for testing and development purposes.

inputFields.name: dependencies
inputFields.description: The dependencies required by this Blok.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: A human-readable description about this Blok's purpose and functionality.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key of this Blok implementation.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: CPUSelector
types.description: Requires CPU resources on the backend.

fields.name: frequency
fields.description: The minimum CPU frequency required, in MHz

types.name: ChoiceInput
types.description:
A choice is a value that can be selected in a dropdown.

It is composed of a value, a label, and a description. The value is the
value that is returned when the choice is selected. The label is the
text that is displayed in the dropdown. The description is the text
that is displayed when the user hovers over the choice.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the choice. This is the text that is displayed in the UI when the user hovers over the choice

inputFields.name: image
inputFields.description: The image of the choice. This is the image that is displayed in the UI (must be a URL)

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: The label of the choice. This is the text that is displayed in the UI

inputFields.name: value
inputFields.description: The value of the choice. This is the value that is returned when the choice is selected

inputFields.name: props

inputFields.name: declaresValue
inputFields.description: If set, this prop declares a new 'value' in the Blok state that can be referenced by other props or actions. The value of this field should be the name of the declared value (e.g., 'selected_user').

inputFields.name: dynamicValue

inputFields.name: key

inputFields.name: staticValue

inputFields.name: utilCall
inputFields.description: Defines an imperative interactive network action callback loop if this prop should trigger a system utility interaction.

types.name: Definition
types.description: An action definition: the abstract, hashed description of an RPC task that a flavour provides.

fields.name: args
fields.description: The input ports (arguments) of this action.

fields.name: collections
fields.description: The collections this action belongs to.

fields.name: definedAt
fields.description: When this action definition was first defined.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A human-readable description of this action.

fields.name: flavours
fields.description: The flavours that provide this action.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: The unique hash identifying this action definition.

fields.name: isTestFor
fields.description: The action definitions that this definition is a test for.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The kind of action, e.g. a function or a generator.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The cleartext name of this action.

fields.name: protocols
fields.description: The protocols this action implements.

fields.name: returns
fields.description: The output ports (return values) of this action.

fields.name: scope
fields.description: The data scope of this action (e.g. local, global or bridge).

fields.name: tests
fields.description: The action definitions that act as tests for this definition.

types.name: DefinitionFilter
types.description: Filter for action definitions.

inputFields.name: demands
inputFields.description: Keep only definitions whose ports satisfy all of the given demands.

inputFields.name: ids
inputFields.description: Keep only definitions whose ID is in this list.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Case-insensitive search on the action name.

types.name: DefinitionInput
types.description:
A definition

    Definitions are the building implementation for Actions and provide the
    information needed to create a action. They are primarly composed of a name,
    a description, and a list of ports.

    Definitions provide a protocol of input and output, and do not contain
    any information about the actual implementation of the action ( this is handled
    by a implementation that implements a action).

inputFields.name: args
inputFields.description: The args of the definition. This is the input ports of the definition

inputFields.name: collections
inputFields.description: The collections of the definition. This is used to group definitions together in the UI

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the definition. This is the text that is displayed in the UI

inputFields.name: interfaces
inputFields.description: The interfaces of the definition. This is used to group definitions together in the UI

inputFields.name: isDev
inputFields.description: Whether the definition is a dev definition or not. If the definition is a dev definition, it can be used to create a dev action. If the definition is not a dev definition, it cannot be used to create a dev action

inputFields.name: isTestFor
inputFields.description: The tests for the definition. This is used to group definitions together in the UI

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key of the definition. This is used to uniquely identify the definition

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of the definition. This is the type of the definition. Can be either a function or a generator

inputFields.name: logo
inputFields.description: The logo of the definition. This is used to display the logo in the UI

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the actions. This is used to uniquely identify the definition

inputFields.name: package
inputFields.description: The package of the function. Will default to the currents agent's app if not specified. This is used to group definitions together in the UI and provide a better user experience

inputFields.name: portGroups
inputFields.description: The port groups of the definition. This is used to group ports together in the UI

inputFields.name: returns
inputFields.description: The returns of the definition. This is the output ports of the definition

inputFields.name: stateful
inputFields.description: Whether the definition is stateful or not. If the definition is stateful, it can be used to create a stateful action. If the definition is not stateful, it cannot be used to create a stateful action

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: The version of the definition. This is used to differentiate if the underyling algorithm has changed, i.e we would expect different results for the same input

types.name: DeletePodInput
types.description: Input for deleting a pod.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The ID of the pod to delete.

types.name: Deployment

fields.name: flavour

fields.name: localId
fields.description: The identifier of this deployment as known to the backend.

fields.name: name

inputFields.description: Keep only deployments whose ID is in this list.

inputFields.name: search
inputFields.description: Case-insensitive search on the deployment name.

fields.name: deviceId
fields.description: The device identifier.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique ID of the device.

types.name: DeviceFeature
types.description: A single hardware feature of a device to match against.

inputFields.name: cpuCount
inputFields.description: The number of CPUs the feature describes.

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of feature (e.g. 'gpu', 'cpu').

types.name: DockerImage
types.description: A reference to a built Docker image.

fields.name: buildAt
fields.description: When this image was built.

fields.name: imageString
fields.description: The fully-qualified image reference (registry/name:tag).

types.name: DumpLogsInput
types.description: Input for attaching a log dump to a pod.

inputFields.name: logs
inputFields.description: The captured log output to store.

inputFields.name: pod
inputFields.description: The ID of the pod the logs belong to.

types.name: DynamicValueInput
types.description: A bound state pointer referencing a variable inside a Blok state instance.

inputFields.name: path
inputFields.description: JSON Pointer to a variable inside the Blok's isolated data model (e.g., '/microscope/exposure').

types.name: EffectClass
types.description: The effect class of an implementation — declared by the implementation, never the caller. NONE work is freely retryable/reclaimable; PHYSICAL work touches the real world (no UPSERT), so an ambiguous failure is terminal and must not be retried.

types.name: EffectInput
types.description:
An effect is a way to modify a port based on a condition. For example,
    you could have an effect that sets a port to null if another port is null.

    Or, you could have an effect that hides the port if another port meets a condition.
    E.g when the user selects a certain option in a dropdown, another port is hidden.

inputFields.name: dependencies
inputFields.description: The dependencies of the effect. Use the .. syntax to traverse the tree of ports. For example, if you have a port with the key 'foo' and you want to reference a port with the key 'bar' that is a child of 'foo', you would use 'foo..bar'

inputFields.name: fade
inputFields.description: Whether to fade out the port when the effect is applied (if it is a hide effect)

inputFields.name: function
inputFields.description: The function to run to determine if the effect should be applied

inputFields.name: hook
inputFields.description: The hook to run when the effect is applied (if it is a custom effect)

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of the effect. Can be either message, hide or custom

inputFields.name: message

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: Identifier
types.description: The `ArrayLike` scalar type represents a reference to a store previously created by the user n a datalayer

types.name: ImplementationInput
types.description: A implementation is a blueprint for a action. It is composed of a definition, a list of dependencies, and a list of params.

inputFields.name: definition
inputFields.description: The definition of the implementation. This is used to uniquely identify the implementation

inputFields.name: dependencies
inputFields.description: The agent dependencies required by this implementation.

inputFields.name: dynamic
inputFields.description: Whether the implementation is dynamic or not. If the implementation is dynamic, it can be used to create a dynamic action. If the implementation is not dynamic, it cannot be used to create a dynamic action

inputFields.name: effect
inputFields.description: The effect class of this implementation. NONE work is freely retryable/reclaimable; PHYSICAL work touches the real world and an ambiguous failure is terminal (never retried). Declared by the implementation here — never by the caller.

inputFields.name: instanceId
inputFields.description: The instance id of the agent this implementation is bound to.

inputFields.name: interface
inputFields.description: The interface of the implementation. This is used to group implementations together in the UI

inputFields.name: locks
inputFields.description: The locks of the implementation. This is used to specify which resources the implementation needs to run

inputFields.name: logo
inputFields.description: The logo of the implementation. This is used to display the logo in the UI either it should be 'custom:svg-paths' or a lucide icon name like 'lucide:activity' urls are not supported at the moment

inputFields.name: manipulates
inputFields.description: The states that the implementation manipulates. This is used to identify which states are manipulated by the implementation, and can be use to enhance state safety in the system

inputFields.name: needsToken
inputFields.description: Whether Rekuest should mint a signed provenance token when this implementation is assigned. Default true (provenance-by-default); set false for trivial/internal tasks that never produce external provenance.

inputFields.name: optimistics
inputFields.description: The optimistics of the definition. This is used to optimistically set state values when the action is assigned, to provide a better user experience.

inputFields.name: params
inputFields.description: The params of the implementation. This is used to pass parameters to the implementation

inputFields.name: provenanceAudience
inputFields.description: The downstream service(s) the provenance token should be scoped to (the token's `aud`). If omitted, Rekuest derives the audience from the structures the assignment acts on.

inputFields.name: tracks
inputFields.description: The tracks of the definition. This is used to track values over time during the runtime of an action. This is the state of a dependency

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: JSONSerializable
types.description: The `JSONSerializable` scalar type represents a JSON-serializable value.

types.name: OptimisticInput
types.description: An optimistic is used to optimistically set state values when the action is assigned. This is used to provide a better user experience by optimistically setting state values when the action is assigned, instead of waiting for the action to be executed and the state to be updated. This will only ever happen on the frontend.

inputFields.name: accessor
inputFields.description: The accessor to get the value to optimistically set. This is used when the value to optimistically set is not the same as the value of the port

inputFields.name: path
inputFields.description: The path to the state.value to optimistically set the value, always traverse from top to bottom level. i.e state.x for state.x and state.x.y for state.x.y. You can also use an arrow function to specify a dynamic path based on the other arguments, e.g. (args) => state[args.foo]

inputFields.name: state
inputFields.description: The state to optimistically set when the action is assigned

types.name: ReturnPortInput
types.description:
Port

    A Port is a single input or output of a action. It is composed of a key and a kind
    which are used to uniquely identify the port.

    If the Port is a structure, we need to define a identifier and scope,
    Identifiers uniquely identify a specific type of model for the scopes (e.g
    all the ports that have the identifier "@mikro/image" are of the same type, and
    are hence compatible with each other). Scopes are used to define in which context
    the identifier is valid (e.g. a port with the identifier "@mikro/image" and the
    scope "local", can only be wired to other ports that have the same identifier and
    are running in the same app). Global ports are ports that have the scope "global",
    and can be wired to any other port that has the same identifier, as there exists a
    mechanism to resolve and retrieve the object for each app. Please check the rekuest
    documentation for more information on how this works.

inputFields.name: children
inputFields.description: The child ports (used for list, dict, union and model ports).

inputFields.name: choices
inputFields.description: The options for the port. This is used for dropdowns and text inputs

inputFields.name: default
inputFields.description: The default value for the port.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the port. This is the text that is displayed in the UI when the user hovers over the port

inputFields.name: effects
inputFields.description: The effects of the port

inputFields.name: identifier
inputFields.description: The identifier of a structure port. This is used to uniquely identify a specific type of structure.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key of the port

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of the port. This is the type of the port. Can be either int, string, structure, list, bool, dict, float, date, union or model

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: The label of the port. This is the text that is displayed in the UI

inputFields.name: nullable
inputFields.description: Whether the port is nullable or not. If the port is nullable, it can be set to null. If the port is not nullable, it cannot be set to null

inputFields.name: provides
inputFields.description: The provisions for the port. Provisions are key-value pairs that can be used to add additional metadata to a port. When using rekuest's action search, you can filter actions based on their port provisions

inputFields.name: validators
inputFields.description: The validators for the port

inputFields.name: widget
inputFields.description: The return widget to use for this port.

types.name: ReturnWidgetInput
types.description:
A Return Widget is a UI element that is used to display the value of a port.

    Return Widgets get displayed both if we show the return values of an assignment,
    but also when we inspect the given arguments of a previous run task. Their primary
    usecase is to adequately display the value of a port, in a user readable way.

    Return Widgets are often overwriten by the underlying UI framework (e.g. Orkestrator)
    to provide a better user experience. For example, a return widget that displays a
    date could be overwriten to display a calendar widget.

    Return Widgets provide more a way to customize this overwriten behavior.

inputFields.name: choices
inputFields.description: The choices to display in the dropdown. This is used for dropdowns and text inputs

inputFields.name: hook
inputFields.description: The hook to run (if it is a custom return widget).

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of the return widget. Can be either dropdown, text, slider, checkbox, radio or custom

inputFields.name: max
inputFields.description: The maximum value to display (if a slider).

inputFields.name: min
inputFields.description: The minimum value to display (if a slider).

inputFields.name: placeholder
inputFields.description: The placeholder text of the return widget.

inputFields.name: query
inputFields.description: The query to run when searching for choices. This is used for dropdowns and text inputs

inputFields.name: step
inputFields.description: The step value to display (if a slider).

inputFields.name: ward
inputFields.description: The ward responsible for handling the return widget.

types.name: ReturnWidgetKind
types.description: The kind of return widget.

types.description: Requires a ROCm-capable (AMD) GPU on the backend.

fields.name: apiThing
fields.description: An additional ROCm capability qualifier

fields.name: apiVersion
fields.description: The minimum ROCm API version required

types.name: SearchQuery
types.description: The `ArrayLike` scalar type represents a reference to a store previously created by the user n a datalayer

types.name: Selector
types.description: A selector expresses a hardware (or capability) requirement that a backend must satisfy to run a flavour.

inputFields.name: apiThing
inputFields.description: An additional ROCm capability qualifier (rocm selectors).

inputFields.name: apiVersion
inputFields.description: The minimum ROCm API version required (rocm selectors).

inputFields.name: cudaCores
inputFields.description: The minimum number of CUDA cores required (cuda selectors).

inputFields.name: frequency
inputFields.description: The minimum CPU frequency required, in MHz (cpu selectors).

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The discriminator identifying which kind of selector this is (e.g. 'cuda', 'rocm', 'cpu', 'oneapi').

inputFields.name: memory
inputFields.description: The minimum memory required, in MB (cpu selectors).

inputFields.name: oneapiVersion
inputFields.description: The minimum oneAPI version required (oneapi selectors).

inputFields.name: optionKey
inputFields.description: The part of the state accessor to use as the value for the assign widget (e.g. the key, the description, the logo, etc.)

inputFields.name: subPath
inputFields.description: The sub path to access a specific part of the state value. Always traverse from top to bottom level. i.e state.x for state.x and state.x.y for state.x.y. You can also use an arrow function to specify a dynamic path based on the other arguments, e.g. (args) => state[args.foo]

types.name: StateDefinitionInput
types.description: A state schema is a blueprint for a state. It is composed of a definition, a list of dependencies, and a list of params.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the state schema. This is used to uniquely identify the state schema

inputFields.name: ports
inputFields.description: The ports of the state schema. This is used to define the structure of the state

types.name: StateDependencyInput
types.description:
A dependency for a implementation. By defining dependencies, you can
    create a dependency graph for your implementations and actions

inputFields.name: allowInactive
inputFields.description: Allow inactive nodes, defaults to true

inputFields.name: app
inputFields.description: Which app this dependency corresponds to (i.e. do you want to use a stardist agent for that or imagej agents needs to be a world unique classsifier (reverse domain notation) that identifies the type of agent you want to use, and then we can have multiple agents of the same type running in the system, e.g. startdist could be the app for all agents that correpsond to a startdist instance)

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: The description of the state. This can describe the state and its purpose.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key of the state. This is used to identify the dependency in the system.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the state. This is used to identify the state in the system.

inputFields.name: optional
inputFields.description: Whether the dependency is optional or not. If the dependency is optional, the agent doesn't have to provide it to be potentially callable

inputFields.name: portMatches
inputFields.description: The demands for the state ports, this can be additionaly specified so that when we loosen the matching criteria for a state in a resolver, we can still make sure to match the right state based on the demands for the ports. This is used to identify the demand in the system.

inputFields.name: protocols
inputFields.description: The protocols that the state is implementing or relying on. This is used to identify the demand in the system, and can be used to match states that are implementing the same protocol together.

inputFields.name: stateKey
inputFields.description: The key of the state this dependency corresponds to. (i.e frame:count)

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: The version of the state this dependency corresponds to.

types.name: StateImplementationInput
types.description: A state implementation is a blueprint for a state. It is composed of a definition, a list of dependencies, and a list of params.

inputFields.name: definition
inputFields.description: The schema of the state implementation. This is used to define the structure of the state

inputFields.name: interface
inputFields.description: The key of the state implementation. This is used to uniquely identify the state implementation

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

fields.name: pod
fields.description: Subscribe to status updates for a single pod.

fields.name: pods
fields.description: Subscribe to status updates for all pods visible to the current organization.

types.name: TrackInput
types.description: A value that is being tracked over time during the runtime of an action. This is the state of a dependency

inputFields.name: dependencyKey
inputFields.description: The key of the dependency whose state is being tracked.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: An optional description for the track.

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: An optional human-readable label for the track.

inputFields.name: stateKey
inputFields.description: The key of the state to track.

inputFields.name: valueKey
inputFields.description: The key of the value within the state to track.

inputFields.name: windows
inputFields.description: The windows (aggregations) computed over the tracked value.

types.name: UntypedParams
types.description: An untyped, free-form JSON object whose shape is not known to the schema.

types.name: UpdateDeploymentInput
types.description: Input for updating a deployment's status.

inputFields.name: deployment
inputFields.description: The ID of the deployment to update.

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: The new status of the deployment.

types.name: UpdatePodInput
types.description: Input for updating a pod's status.

inputFields.name: localId
inputFields.description: The backend-local identifier of the pod to update.

inputFields.name: pod
inputFields.description: The ID of the pod to update; required unless 'local_id' is given.

inputFields.name: status
inputFields.description: The new status of the pod.

types.name: User
types.description: Represents an authenticated user.

fields.name: sub
fields.description: The subject identifier of the user.

types.name: UtilCallInput
types.description: Defines a utility call that can be invoked within the system.

inputFields.name: arguments
inputFields.description: Key-value arguments map compiled for the target utility call.

inputFields.name: operation
inputFields.description: The utility function name to invoke.

types.name: ValidatorFunction
types.description:
The `Validator` scalar represents a javascript function that should execute on the client side (inside a shadow realm)
      to validate a value (or a set of values) before it is sent to the server.  The function has two parameters (value, otherValues) and should return a string if the value is invalid and undefined if the value is valid.
        The otherValues parameter is an object with the other values in the form {fieldName: value}.

types.name: ValidatorInput
types.description:
A validating function for a port. Can specify a function that will run when validating values of the port.
If outside dependencies are needed they need to be specified in the dependencies field. With the .. syntax
when transversing the tree of ports.

inputFields.name: dependencies
inputFields.description: The dependencies of the function. Use the .. syntax to traverse the tree of ports. For example, if you have a port with the key 'foo' and you want to reference a port with the key 'bar' that is a child of 'foo', you would use 'foo..bar'

inputFields.name: errorMessage
inputFields.description: The error message to display when the validation fails

inputFields.name: function
inputFields.description: The function to run when validating the port

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: An optional human-readable label for the validator.

types.name: WindowInput
types.description: A window that is calculated

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: An optional human-readable label for the window.

inputFields.name: windowFunction
inputFields.description: The window function to apply over the tracked value.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

enumValues.name: DIRECTIVE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a directive definition.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
